methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $270M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
REGENT Craft is a Series A-stage transportation company founded by Billy Thalheimer and Mike Klinker, currently valued at $270M post-money after raising $60M to date. The target operates within a Transportation sector comp universe of 8 private companies (including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and FleetZero) and 0 public peers.

The valuation analysis applies one triangulation method due to data constraints. Stage-Progression methodology is applicable and yields a $540M implied valuation, derived by applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $270M last post-money valuation. Peer-Median analysis is not applicable: while 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, the comparable set lacks sufficient depth—zero same-stage matches, zero adjacent-stage matches, and only one similar-tier comparable—falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable peer benchmarking. Broadening to all-sector medians would introduce undue noise given the specialized nature of frontier transportation technologies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $459M (low) to $621M (high), centered on a $540M median. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology; key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific subsector, the lack of direct peer comparables at the same development stage, and the absence of public market reference points in frontier transportation. Investors should treat this range as preliminary pending acquisition of additional comparable data or financial metrics from the target.
